item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think finding a replacement image for this one would be incredibly easy. You could even edit it to be black and white.

item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Image belongs to robin, under All Rights Reserved. As such, it has since been removed.

item.title: Re: Very good
item.content:encoded:
I think that it MIGHT have something to do with forgetting details about it, but I have NO clue.
Translation: It is an object or entity whose true nature is a mystery. According to its file: "SCP-055 is a "self-keeping secret" or "anti-meme". Information about SCP-055's physical appearance as well as its nature, behavior, and origins is self-classifying."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks very much

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's a section of Visions of a Better World.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wasn't there a tale or log entry about a guy who enters this and gains knowledge of how to take over the world? I've been looking but cant find it, anyone know what I'm talking about or am I going mad?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This SCP can provide one with knowledge on where all your socks go after you put them through the wash.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: caseless ammunition already existed though, it was mainly used for the HK G11 rifle

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fingers crossed I don't suddenly know what being drowned in every naturally occuring liquid in the universe feels like.

I'd prefer maybe… Hmm.
How about Gods's middle name?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *exposes self to SCP 028, suddenly is god* Huh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: /thumbs up for proper Latin plural of addendum

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We need a whole page dedicated to 028's addenda

+1

item.title: Re: Very good
item.content:encoded: I don't get it. Where's the reference?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Geological structure of the earth beneath Greenland, including several unknown caves and [EXPUNGED]

This line seems like a good fit for a wikiwalk crosslink to SCP-722, since it is "coiled through a series of tunnels of unknown origin" in "the eastern glacial ranges of Greenland". Sure, it's another Series I skip, but it doesn't seem to get much attention, and the link is a natural fit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 Put him with the D class from the game.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What is it with SCPs and keys to a Buick LeSabre?

item.title: Re: Comment #4 to Make Gears Happy
item.content:encoded: actually? that's really neat.

item.title: Re: Staff Post- Open
item.content:encoded:
Oh I totally agree that giving someone random knowledge of something is likely to mean very bad things. I simply prefer it being left to the reader's imagination what our Agent gained (like what happened to D-1182: we don't and will never know what they learned.)

That being said, I've been here for about a week and you're staff, so who am I to judge?

item.title: Re: Staff Post- Open
item.content:encoded: I think that bit is good because it reinforces one of the main fridge logic bits of this article, that using it could cause any of the Foundations deepest darkest secrets to be known by some joe schmoe, and with the number of infohazardous SCPs out there alone that can be really dangerous.

item.title: Re: Staff Post- Open
item.content:encoded:
Personally, I prefer the original in this case. That being said, worldproblems left me an I N T E R P E R T A T I O N junkie so I may be biased.

e1: although the "Any Foundation personnel found to have entered SCP-028's area of effect are to be detained indefinitely." bit is good. keep it.

item.title: Staff Post- Open
item.content:encoded:
e: Timer removed after edit was made. The original author may revert or change these edits at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 72 hours if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ show block
– hide block

The blackboxed SCP number needs to be filled in by something requiring a lot of energy to contain. I suggest SCP-1995, since cold fusion might be useful in a kinetic energy-cancelling temperature-sensitive energy-abundant anomaly.

Editing this passage:

E-127: Agent ███████ accidentally exposed to SCP-028. Agent showed signs of sudden surprise and bemusement. When questioned, Agent requested “a moment to gather my thoughts, please”. After several seconds, Agent laughed, shook his head, and removed his service pistol from its holster. Agent then shot and wounded Dr. ████, and killed Agents ████████ and █████, before turning the gun on himself.

To read:

E-127: Agent ███████ accidentally exposed to SCP-028. Agent showed signs of sudden surprise and bemusement. When questioned, Agent requested “a moment to gather my thoughts, please”. After several seconds, Agent laughed, shook his head, and removed his service pistol from its holster. Agent then shot and wounded Dr. ████, and killed Agents ████████ and █████, before being restrained. Post-incident interrogation revealed Agent ███████ had extensive knowledge of classified Foundation activities, and several SCP objects he had not been previously exposed to, including SCP-2669.

Any Foundation personnel found to have entered SCP-028's area of effect are to be detained indefinitely.

Trying to make the angle of "Foundation personnel learn things they might not like" and to reinforce that there's an edit to an SCPs where people have been treated poorly by the Foundation. Plus 2669 itself has a crosslink to project heimdall.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
LOL "How the hell did he do that?"

Great question

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is probably my favorite SCP from block one, tbh. The second to last two tests are cheap and obvious, but goddamn if they don't tickle me in the right way for some reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, but this is a very old article- that's simply an artifact of how things used to be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You are right. Writing the numbers with letters is also unnecessary. However, this kind of writing was common in the early days of the site, and the old SCPs are preserved like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"SCP-028 is sealed in a six (6) by six (6) by three (3) meter (twenty (20) by twenty (20) by ten (10) foot) concrete room with a single"

I don't think that the foot measurements are necessary

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+400.

I wish there was more, but what is there is very… nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Surprised that the D-class who learned how to not breathe and live wasn't kept alive for more studies. Might even qualify for the designation of SCP-028-A, or even his own SCP number.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh! Sorry. Will refrain from suggesting similar things in the future. Probably should refrain from offering advice entirely until I've finished reading through the archives, actually. Thanks for pointing that out to me!

EDIT: In retrospect, this comes off as really passive-aggressive. That was not how I intended it; I'm sorry about that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Might be worth adding a reference to some form of punishment for the researcher who got two Agents killed.

That isn't something we've really done around here for quite a long time, and it's no longer in keeping with general site standards. This isn't very good advice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Someone didn't get the "don't arm test subjects, and also don't stand too close to test subjects" memo. Might be worth adding a reference to some form of punishment for the researcher who got two Agents killed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I as well agree. Accidental Necro-post fuck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aye

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All in favor of an collaboration experiment log?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I disagree. This would lose some of its value if it became a thing what makes you good at something. It does better as something that just needs to be contained, not something of any inherent use to the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would love to see more experiments as well maybe even make a log for collaboration

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this page, and I love the fact that the SCP is simply an area in which this effect takes place, one thing bothers me… A few times, the "Effect" of the SCP is spelled with a capital letter, and a few times with a lowercase letter. I don't really understand the significance, but it might be better to use entirely a lowercase E or an uppercase one, just for consistency.

But that's just a nitpick; I still love it. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It'd be neat if the knowledge gained wasn't completely random, the coal miner found more efficient energy, and out of all the information in the world, why did someone under foundation observation learn everything about 12 SCPs? It can't be coincidence! +1 and would love to see more experiments

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad I found this again. One of those articles that left a huge impression on me when I joined a year ago.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm surprised there haven't been more horrifying facts, considering the 'verse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I knew about this one for some time so it is time to give credit where credit is due.
I love this SCP. It is one of my all-time favorites, simply because of the simplicity. The interesting narrative written around it is convincing and it's all put together nicely.
I have a thing for simple things that actually work, unlike those huge novel-sized scp's which really draw me away and confuse me.

item.title: Re: Very good
item.content:encoded: Wait, there's a 55?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd love to see more experiment logs for this, those really sold it for me! The original description of the procedures felt a bit awkward to me though, like I didn't really get what this thing was or how it was contained, no matter how many times I read it. :/
I just learned about this event today. I unfortunately have no idea when it happened. 028 is fickle like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is now one of my favorite SCPs, but I feel like there should be a page for experiment logs, that anyone could add to.

item.title: Re: Mod Post
item.content:encoded: Updated.

item.title: Re: Mod Post
item.content:encoded: I agree as well.

item.title: Re: Mod Post
item.content:encoded: I agree.

item.title: Mod Post
item.content:encoded: Yes, I think it should. With modly assent, I'd like to change "photographic" to "eidetic".

item.title: Use of photographic in regards to memory.
item.content:encoded: If the result is complete and total knowledge/recall shouldn't it be referred to as eidetic instead of photographic?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Guys, we're not going to have the tech to kill that thing for like, a bajillion years. It would probably save time if we just let it kill us all now."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Throw D-class at it till someone knows everything about 682.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the SCP that made me fall in love with the site. Just so random, spooky, funny…everything I've come to enjoy about the site, in one neat little SCP.

E-112, E-127 and Experiment-189 are perfect. Any SCP that has seriously random qualities to it should take a page from Knowledge.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The fusion reactor by itself (probably) isn't the issue - more likely it's "How did you figure this out?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-028 came to the Foundation’s attention after research into news reports of a local miner who submitted a design for a cold-fusion reactor to the US patent office. … News and subjects were suppressed and contained after discovery of SCP-028

Why would the Foundation suppress knowledge of a practical fusion reactor? Did it operate via paranormal means?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first I was going to let this one be because it was just interesting, but when I read the prank in SCP-50 I had to upvote.

Guy 1: I Know, I Know!, I KNOW!

Guy 2: Know what?

Guy 1: (Looks up and stares deeply into guy 2's eyes and whispers) EVERYTHING!

XD Freaking genius

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Location of keys for a Buick LeSabre

so im guessing it can give you knowledge of something that can change but the knoledge wont

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If it gives you knowledge of something that could change, like say the names of all Billboard Top 100 songs or something, would your knowledge change to fit, or would it simply stay at whatever knowledge you were given when you entered the area? Or does it only give you knowledge of things that are unchanging?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Indeed. Happy +100, article!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's not.

It just hit triple digits.
